introSection:
Kirnu (Finnish for "Churn") is a steel 4th dimension roller coaster located at Linnanmäki in Helsinki, Uusimaa, Finland. It was manufactured by Intamin and on-site construction was handled by RCS GmbH.

Opened in 2007, the ride is the first ZacSpin installation from Intamin. It is located between the Vekkula (funhouse) and Linnanmäki's performance stage.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Kirnu has a short layout, with no lateral turns. The cars spin, and the amount of spinning is affected by rider's weight.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: There are 4 riders on each side of the track, with half facing forwards and the other half facing backwards. The 4 seats on each side of the track rotate independently from the opposite side.

fullText:
Kirnu (Finnish for "Churn") is a steel 4th dimension roller coaster located at Linnanmäki in Helsinki, Uusimaa, Finland. It was manufactured by Intamin and on-site construction was handled by RCS GmbH.

Opened in 2007, the ride is the first ZacSpin installation from Intamin. It is located between the Vekkula (funhouse) and Linnanmäki's performance stage.

Kirnu has a short layout, with no lateral turns. The cars spin, and the amount of spinning is affected by rider's weight.

There are 4 riders on each side of the track, with half facing forwards and the other half facing backwards. The 4 seats on each side of the track rotate independently from the opposite side.
